## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so now we're going to talk about the list getting process. And so the way this works, it's really pretty simple. What you do for the list getting process is you have some kind of a bribe. And then you have some traffic sources. Hopefully multiple traffic sources. And you send that traffic into the bribe. And then to get the bribe, they have to go through this process where they end up on your list. Okay, now one really important thing you have to do is you have to decide what you're going to publish about. What niche you're going to build your list and you're going to build your business in. And then of course once you figure that out, then you start to work on what the bribe is. So that's sort of what we're going to be talking about here. So first of all, let's talk about the niche. I don't know what's up with that, but in any case, lots of people who are going to be watching this, you already know what market you're in. You've already got your business, you're already building your list. You just want to learn how to build it better and faster. But a lot of people are still probably trying to figure out what niche they're going to publish in. And a lot of people started with this. For example, I'll give you one prime example, me. I know when I first started out, I first learned about the idea of information marketing. This is actually before the internet was really around. I learned about publishing on CompuServe and AOL and Prodigy. It was way back in the dark ages. Then by the time I started to figure out what my niche was, then the internet really had come about. So dark age. But this is the deal. Back then, I was studying the stock market a lot. Like every day, I wanted to be a trader. I wanted to be a trade the stock market for a living. So I would study the market. I was doing all kinds of computer modeling and just obsessed about the stock market. Just putting all my time into the market, trying to figure it out. Trying to figure out how to trade it. Trying to figure out different ways of predicting what would happen with prices. And then I learned about this information marketing. So what I would do is I'd sit there and for like 10 hours a day, I would study the stock market. Then I'd go to bed. And while I was laying in bed, sort of like that half hour, laying down in bed would be the time I'd think about my, the idea of an information marketing business about publishing and about having an email list. And I remember laying in bed, I was like, what could I publish about? What could my niche be?

## [03:01 - 06:02]

I want to publish something. I don't know what it's going to be. The next morning I would get up and I'd study the stock market for like 8 or 10 hours. I do know a little bit about publishing via email, but it took me like 18 months when it was just obvious, like right in front of my face, what I should be publishing about. I couldn't figure it out. So if you're struggling, you know, give yourself a break. Lots of people struggle with this. There's a couple of different ways you can go. You can either publish about something you love or you can just be a complete mercenary and find a good market to publish in. Neither one of those is the right answer for everyone. There are a lot of people that care about that. But, you know, the eating pizza, I'm probably not going to get paid very much if I publish about eating pizza. So what I'm saying is some people are naturally lucky to have things they're interested in to publish about that they love. And frankly, I love publishing about internet marketing. That really, I love helping entrepreneurs. So I am lucky in that something I've really loved to do also pays off. ## [06:02 - 09:03]

But, I mean, if you went to Google and, you know, there's the big search box and you type in growing tomatoes, then you're going to get all these, you know, all the websites are going to come up here, you know, on the page. And then over here there's going to be a column with more stuff here. And these are the ads, the Google AdWord ads. And sometimes there's a slightly different color box up here with another listing. You can look and see if a lot of people are, if people are advertising in the niche, then people have to be buying stuff in that niche. So that's one of the, you know, I don't have time to go into a full-out keyword, you know, discussion keyword tutorial here. But this is, you look and see if people are advertising in your market or your niche. And if people are advertising, then people are spending money there. Another thing you can do is go to a good, like, bookstore or a store where they sell newspapers and magazines. There's always going to be a major city, some great store that's got tons and tons of magazines. Look at the magazines in your niche. Are there magazines? How tightly targeted are they? Like, is it just about growing tomatoes or just about growing vegetables? Is it growing certain types of tomatoes? So if you look at what people are paying, and in ad words you can take it a step further and look at actually how much people are paying. All, this is all very easy to do compared to a few years ago. So you want to look into your niche and try to narrow down into a niche that you can publish in and find out what the competitors are and what they're paying for traffic.
